digest.title:
LP Mastermind
Market Update
digest.subtitle: What moved this week, what it means for your ranges, and what's worth talking about.
digest.intro: BTC just cracked a 7-month downtrend ceiling even as spot pulled back 2% on the day, while ETH underperformed again on a relative basis. For concentrated LPs -- whether you run tight sub-5% bands or wider asymmetric ranges -- that divergence plus a fresh $292M bridge exploit means range discipline and pair selection matter more than directional conviction this week.

stories.title: Kelp rsETH Bridge Drained for ~$292M
stories.body: A LayerZero-based exploit on Kelp DAO's rsETH bridge pulled roughly $292M before the emergency multisig paused core contracts 46 minutes later. rsETH and adjacent LRT pairs will see depeg volatility, thin books, and wide spreads across V3/V4 pools. If you're running tight ranges on rsETH/ETH or rsETH/wstETH, you're the exit liquidity for every JIT bot front-running the depeg -- pull now or accept you're subsidizing arb flow at a loss. Wider-range LPs have more cushion but still need to mark down the peg anchor and price in a permanent risk premium until redemption mechanics and backing are publicly reconciled.
stories.spark: Are you exiting LRT pairs entirely after this, or widening ranges and pricing in a permanent risk premium on the fee tier?

stories.title: BTC Breaks 7-Month Downtrend Despite Red Day
stories.body: Decrypt flags BTC clearing a seven-month ceiling with $84K cited as the next magnet, even as spot printed -2.2% on the session. If you're running tight sub-5% bands on WBTC/ETH or WBTC/USDC V3, watch out -- this is the worst possible regime for your strategy. Breakout-on-weekly with chop-on-daily means your ranges get picked off on both sides of the retest, you bleed gas on every reposition, and you end up on the wrong side of each whipsaw into the $84K magnet. Either step aside through the retest or move to V4 hooks with dynamic fees that actually price the churn. If you're running wider ranges with asymmetric skew to the upside, this is exactly where you earn your keep -- you give up fee density until price settles into a direction, but you avoid the reposition tax and capture the trend when it resolves.
stories.spark: Are you repositioning WBTC ranges for a $84K target, or staying neutral until the retest confirms?

stories.title: ETH Underperformance Continues as XRP Lands on Solana
stories.body: ETH fell 3.2% against a 2.2% BTC drop, and wXRP crossing $1.2M minted on Solana is another quiet signal that non-ETH L1 liquidity keeps fragmenting. For tight-range LPs on ETH/USDC, persistent relative weakness means symmetric bands around spot will drift out-of-range downward and leave you holding 100% ETH at the worst time -- skew bands below spot or accept that you're accumulating on the way down. Wider-range LPs can hold symmetric positions but should shift ratios stable-heavy to reflect the grind lower on the numeraire. V4 hooks that auto-rebalance toward the stable leg are worth a serious look at size for either camp.
stories.spark: What ETH/stable ratio are you targeting in your ranges given the persistent underperformance vs BTC?

stories.title: RaveDAO Pump Investigation Opens
stories.body: Binance and Bitget opened probes into the RAVE token after ZachXBT alleged a coordinated pump-and-dump and put up a $25K whistleblower bounty. Expect forced delistings or trading halts, which cascade into CEX/DEX arbitrage gaps and sudden LP drawdowns on any V3 pool holding RAVE. Tight-range LPs on low-float names like this see catastrophic single-sided fills when a halt hits -- you wake up holding 100% of the worthless leg. Wider ranges soften the hit but don't eliminate it, because the liquidity underneath simply disappears on exit. This is the recurring tax on providing liquidity to low-float, celebrity-adjacent tokens -- worth auditing your long tail positions for anything with similar launch fingerprints.
stories.spark: What's your hard filter for avoiding pools like this -- float, insider concentration, listing venue, or something else?

stories.title: Q-Day and the Long-Tail Risk to LP Collateral
stories.body: Decrypt's Q-Day piece revisits the quantum threat to Bitcoin signatures, and while it's not a 2026 problem, it's a reminder that LP strategies sitting on multi-year WBTC exposure inherit the underlying cryptographic risk. Any bridge-wrapped BTC or long-dated LP NFT is effectively a bet that migration paths exist before the threat matures. Tight-range LPs rotate positions frequently enough that the exposure window is short, so the risk is mostly to the underlying collateral rather than the position itself. Wider-range LPs holding the same NFT for quarters at a time carry both the collateral risk and the inertia of not rebalancing -- worth factoring into position sizing. Not actionable this quarter, but it belongs in the same mental bucket as bridge risk and oracle risk.
stories.spark: Does quantum risk change how you think about multi-year LP lockups, or is it still too far out to price?

digest.closing: Bridge exploits and celebrity memecoin probes in the same week -- what's the one risk control you're tightening before next Monday's open?
